title: Badge Tailgating and Anti-Passback Violations
spl:
index=physical sourcetype="access_control"
| where match(event_subtype,"(?i)tailgate|apb|anti.passback")
| stats count by door, badge_holder, reader_id
| sort -count
description: Tracks piggyback events where anti-passback rules fire or two entries occur without intermediate exit‚Äîstronger signal than generic door events alone.
value: Tracks piggyback events where anti-passback rules fire or two entries occur without intermediate exit‚Äîstronger signal than generic door events alone.
implementation: Enable anti-passback on mantraps where supported. Correlate with video analytics if available. Escalate repeat doors.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
‚Ä¢ Install and configure the required add-on or app: Access control system.
‚Ä¢ Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="access_control"` (event_subtype=tailgate, apb_violation).
‚Ä¢ For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 ‚Äî Configure data collection
Enable anti-passback on mantraps where supported. Correlate with video analytics if available. Escalate repeat doors.

Step 2 ‚Äî Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=physical sourcetype="access_control"
| where match(event_subtype,"(?i)tailgate|apb|anti.passback")
| stats count by door, badge_holder, reader_id
| sort -count
```

Understanding this SPL

**Badge Tailgating and Anti-Passback Violations** ‚Äî Tracks piggyback events where anti-passback rules fire or two entries occur without intermediate exit‚Äîstronger signal than generic door events alone.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="access_control"` (event_subtype=tailgate, apb_violation). **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Access control system. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed‚Äîrename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: physical; **sourcetype**: access_control. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

‚Ä¢ Scopes the data: index=physical, sourcetype="access_control". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
‚Ä¢ Filters the current rows with `where match(event_subtype,"(?i)tailgate|apb|anti.passback")` ‚Äî typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.
‚Ä¢ `stats` rolls up events into metrics; results are split **by door, badge_holder, reader_id** so each row reflects one combination of those dimensions (useful for per-host, per-user, or per-entity comparisons for this use case).
‚Ä¢ Orders rows with `sort` ‚Äî combine with `head`/`tail` for top-N patterns.


Step 3 ‚Äî Validate
Confirm that events are present in the index and that the search returns expected results. Compare with known good/bad scenarios if applicable. Verify field extractions and index permissions.

Step 4 ‚Äî Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (tailgate events), Bar chart (by door), Timeline.
visualization: Table (tailgate events), Bar chart (by door), Timeline.
